data.title: US Treasury Secretary's strong dollar policy fails to stem decline; currency devaluation concerns dominate market.
data.description: The dollar fell in Asian trading on Thursday, halting Wednesday's rebound, as renewed concerns about a potential dollar depreciation outweighed Treasury Secretary Bessant's reaffirmation of a strong dollar policy. "The new Bond King," Gundlach, stated that the dollar has not acted as a safe-haven currency for some time, as investors prefer tangible assets such as precious metals. Damien Loh, chief investment officer and co-founder of hedge fund Ensemble Capital, said, "Bessant used 'strategic ambiguity' when talking about the dollar." He explained, "A comprehensive weak dollar policy like the one Trump initially advocated could become self-reinforcing and get out of control, so Bessant is trying to mitigate that expectation, although I think they still want the dollar to weaken gradually." (Jinshi)

data.title: European Central Bank to Accept DLT-Issued Assets as Collateral
data.description: The European Central Bank (ECB) will begin accepting tradeable assets issued using distributed ledger technology (DLT) within central securities depositories (CSD) as eligible collateral for Eurosystem credit operations starting March 30. According to Foresight News, these assets must meet the Eurosystem's collateral eligibility criteria and management requirements, including settlement in securities settlement systems that comply with CSD regulations and can connect to TARGET2-Securities (T2S). The initiative will be implemented in phases, taking into account market developments and the evolution of related legal frameworks such as MiCAR and the DLT pilot regime. This move aims to encourage innovation and promote the integration of European capital markets.

data.title: Australian Court Fines BPS Financial for Misleading Practices
data.description: Australia's Federal Court has imposed a fine of 14 million AUD on BPS Financial, the operator of Qoin Wallet, for operating without a financial services license. According to Foresight News, the court found that over the past three years, the company engaged in misleading and deceptive conduct regarding the use of Qoin tokens for merchant transactions and their exchangeability for cash or other digital assets. Joe Longo, Chairman of the Australian Securities and Investments Commission (ASIC), stated that the penalty aims to send a strong deterrent message to the digital asset industry.

data.title: Dollar Declines Amid Renewed Devaluation Concerns
data.description: The dollar's exchange rate fell during Thursday's Asian trading session, halting its rebound from Wednesday. According to ChainCatcher, concerns over a potential devaluation of the dollar have resurfaced, overshadowing U.S. Treasury Secretary Besant's reaffirmation of a strong dollar policy. Damien Loh, Chief Investment Officer at hedge fund Ensemble Capital, noted that Besant employed 'strategic ambiguity' when discussing the dollar, aiming to temper market expectations of a comprehensive weak dollar policy.

data.title: 0G and American Fortress launch AI-native private and compliant transaction infrastructure
data.description: 0G announced a partnership with AmericanFortress to launch a private and compliant transaction infrastructure for AI agents, aiming to fill the critical gap in trusted identity and privacy transaction layers within the AI ​​agent economy. 0G stated that as AI agents increasingly execute on-chain transactions, manage vaults, and interact with DeFi protocols, existing transaction architectures based on plaintext addresses and lacking privacy protection have exposed significant security and compliance risks. The report states that the solution involves 0G providing the AI ​​agent computation layer, while AmericanFortress provides a one-time transaction mechanism based on dynamically anonymous addresses. This allows humans and AI agents to transfer value using names rather than public on-chain addresses, reducing risks such as address poisoning and transaction hijacking, and ensuring transaction uncorrelation for third-party observers. Simultaneously, this infrastructure strengthens privacy while supporting cryptographic proofs and decentralized identity verification, accommodating compliant disclosure requirements, and is designed for large-scale machine-generated transaction scenarios.

data.title: U.S. Government Shutdown Negotiations Show Progress Amid Funding Discussions
data.description:
Negotiations between U.S. President Donald Trump and Senate Minority Leader Schumer are advancing to prevent a government shutdown. According to BlockBeats, initial plans involve separating Homeland Security funding from the appropriations bill, while legislation for military and health projects is expected to pass before the Friday midnight deadline.

Congress is considering a short-term funding extension for Homeland Security operations to prevent service disruptions and allow time for drafting new legislation.

Market predictions indicate positive impacts from the negotiations. Polymarket data shows that as of January 29, 2026, at 12:54, the probability of a U.S. government shutdown this Saturday has sharply decreased to 40%, marking a 29% drop within the day.

data.title: Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $19.6449 million yesterday, with BlackRock's IBIT experiencing the largest net outflow at $14.1792 million.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Bitcoin spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $19.6449 million yesterday (January 28th, Eastern Time). The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net inflow was the Fidelity ETF (FBTC), with a net inflow of $19.451 million. FBTC's historical total net inflow has reached $11.434 billion. The Bitcoin spot ETF with the largest single-day net outflow was the BlackRock ETF (IBIT), with a net outflow of $14.1792 million. IBIT's historical total net inflow has reached $62.802 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Bitcoin spot ETFs is $115.351 billion, with an ETF net asset ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Bitcoin's total market capitalization) of 6.48%. The historical cumulative net inflow has reached $56.334 billion.

data.title: Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $28.0951 million yesterday, with BlackRock's ETHA ETF leading the way with a net inflow of $27.3431 million.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, Ethereum spot ETFs saw a total net inflow of $28.0951 million yesterday (January 28th, Eastern Time). The BlackRock ETF (ETHA) saw the largest single-day net inflow of $27.3431 million, bringing its historical total net inflow to $12.454 billion. The Fidelity ETF (FETH) followed with a net inflow of $752,000, bringing its historical total net inflow to $2.729 billion. As of press time, the total net asset value of Ethereum spot ETFs is $18.223 billion, with an ETF net asset value ratio (market capitalization as a percentage of Ethereum's total market capitalization) of 5.01%, and a cumulative historical net inflow of $12.383 billion.

data.title: Yi Lihua: Short sellers overemphasize the four-year cycle; the key is to grasp the main trend of the bull market.
data.description: Liquid Capital founder Yi Lihua stated in an article on the X platform that some short sellers are using a "four-year cycle" as their main logic, believing that 2025 has entered a bear market phase similar to 2019-2020, with the market potentially experiencing prolonged sideways movement or even undergoing a shakeout through extreme volatility. He pointed out that Trend Research had previously positioned itself at Ethereum's low point and liquidated its positions during the high-level consolidation phase, and these operations have been verified on-chain. Yi Lihua emphasized that the team will not change its established strategy due to sentiment or short-term noise; the core objective is to grasp the main trend in the long-term cycle of the crypto market, avoid missing bull market opportunities, and thus achieve long-term returns.

data.title: Liquid Capital Founder Discusses Market Trends and Strategy
data.description:
Liquid Capital founder Yi Lihua recently shared insights on the X platform regarding market trends. According to Odaily, some bearish investors are using the 'four-year cycle' as a primary logic, suggesting that 2025 might resemble the bear market phase of 2019-2020, with the potential for prolonged sideways trading or extreme volatility.

Yi highlighted that Trend Research strategically positioned itself during Ethereum's low phase and exited during high volatility, with these actions verified on-chain. He emphasized that the team remains committed to its established strategy, focusing on capturing major trends in the crypto market's long cycles to avoid missing bull market opportunities and achieve long-term gains.
